questions.question: West Bengal has signed a new ‘Reciprocal Transport Agreement’ with which state?
questions.explanation: Sikkim and West Bengal Government recently signed a new reciprocal transport agreement. As per the agreement, ‘All-Bengal’ permits for Sikkim vehicles and ‘All-Sikkim’ permits for Bengal vehicles will be issued to facilitate vehicular movement across both the states. Previously the vehicles had limited permits for movement across the other states.

questions.question: Which is set to become the first state in the country to have an exclusive Research and Development (R&D) Policy?
questions.explanation: Karnataka is set to become the first state in the country to have an exclusive Research and Development (R&D) Policy. The policy seeks to allocate at least 0.1 per cent of its Gross State Domestic Product towards R&D and innovation. Karnataka is the first state to have an exclusive aerospace and defence R&D policy. It also has a separate semiconductor and renewable energy policy.

questions.question: As per the recent notification by the Finance Ministry, what is the GST applied for sale of the Indian national flag?
questions.explanation: As per the recent notification by the Finance Ministry, sale of the Indian national flag is exempt from the Goods and Services Tax (GST). The Indian national flag, irrespective of whether machine made or of polyester, is exempt from the Goods and Services Tax (GST). Hand-woven, hand-spun national flags made of cotton, silk, wool or Khadi are already exempt from GST.

questions.question: Singalila National Park, which was seen in the news recently, is situated in which state?
questions.explanation: The Singalila National Park, the highest protected area in West Bengal has launched a programme to release about 20 red pandas in a period of five years. West Bengal’s Padmaja Naidu Himalayan Zoological Park has started this programme to release 20 of red pandas (Ailurus fulgens) into the forests.

questions.question: India achieved a world record for construction of longest Double Decker Viaduct in which city?
questions.explanation: India achieved a world record for construction of longest Double Decker Viaduct in Nagpur with 3.14 kilo meters. The Maharashtra Metro and NHAI also constructed the highest number of metro stations on the Double Decker Viaduct, which has been recognized by Asia Book of Records and India Book of Records.

questions.question: Who has been named the captain of the Indian women’s cricket team in the 2022 Commonwealth Games?
questions.explanation: Harmanpreet Kaur has been named captain of the Indian women’s team, which is set to play at the 2022 Commonwealth Games, Birmingham. Opener Smriti Mandhana was named as the deputy captain. This is the first time Women’s T20 cricket is participating in the Commonwealth Games after a men’s ODI tournament was played at the 1998 Kuala Lumpur Commonwealth Games.

questions.question: ‘Kharchi’ is a traditional festival is celebrated in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: ‘Kharchi’ is a week-long traditional festival, in which prayer to 14 Gods and Goddess is offered with religious fervour. The festival began in presence of thousands of devotees from across the country at Khayerpur, Tripura. It started with the bathing procession of the deities at River Haora.

questions.question: Which neighbouring country of India recently passed its first Citizenship Amendment Bill?
questions.explanation: Nepal’s Parliament passed the country’s first Citizenship Amendment Bill, which was under discussion for more than two years. The bill has been tabled in parliament to amend the Nepal Citizenship Act 2006 and make provisions for providing citizenship as directed by the Constitution.

questions.question: Which is the headquarters of ‘International Rice Research Institute’ (IRRI)?
questions.explanation: The International Rice Research Institute is an international agricultural research and training organization with its headquarters in Los Baños, Laguna in the Philippines. It was founded in 1960. India’s Department of Agriculture & Farmer Welfare (DA&FW) has signed a Memorandum of Agreement (MoA) with the International Rice Research Institute (IRRI) for Phase -2 activities of IRRI South Asia Regional Centre (ISARC).

questions.question: ‘Right to Repair’ framework, which was seen in the news, is associated with which Ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution

questions.explanation: The Union government is formulating “Right to Repair” framework to cut down practices introduced by some manufacturers so that people can get goods such as consumer durables, phones and cars fixed on their own. Union Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution announced that a government committee, which was set up to develop the comprehensive framework on the Right to Repair, held its first meeting recently and identified important sectors to implement it.
